title: A Millennium Prize Problem solved by end of 2027
question_text: Will a solution to any of the six unsolved Clay Millennium Prize Problems (P vs NP, Riemann Hypothesis, Hodge Conjecture, Yang-Mills existence & mass gap, Navier-Stokes existence & smoothness, Birch-Swinnerton-Dyer) be published and gain broad acceptance in the mathematical community on or before 2027-12-31?
resolution_criteria: Resolves YES if, on or before 2027-12-31, a proof (or disproof/counterexample) of one of the six unsolved Clay Millennium Prize Problems is published and recognized by expert consensus as correct/resolving the problem. A merely claimed or unverified preprint does NOT count; genuine community acceptance is required, but a formal Clay Prize award is NOT (its 2-year waiting period would make a 2027 resolution impossible). Resolves NO otherwise.
resolution_source: Mathematical community consensus as reflected in expert commentary, major mathematics venues/journals, and Clay Mathematics Institute statements
edge_cases: The Poincare Conjecture (resolved ~2003) is excluded. A partial result or special case does not count. A disputed or not-yet-verified proof resolves NO; if a claim is genuinely unresolvable by the deadline it resolves NO (not VOID, since 'not accepted by the deadline' is a clean NO). Only the six listed problems count.
